name: TitleMax Title Pawns
description_short: TitleMax Title Pawns at 3665 Flat Shoals Rd Suite B, Decatur, GA 30034 is Mon-Fri 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 5.0 stars across 573 reviews. Call +1 404-243-7345.
description_long:
TitleMax was founded in 1998 in Georgia and has grown into one of the largest title lending companies in the United States. Operated under parent company TMX Finance LLC, headquartered at 15 Bull Street, Savannah, GA, TitleMax runs 900+ stores across 14 states under three brands: TitleMax, TitleBucks, and InstaLoan. The company holds no CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications and is not a bank or credit union. In Texas, it operates as a licensed Credit Access Business (CAB) and Credit Services Organization (CSO), brokering loans through a third-party lender. TitleMax is subject to federal CFPB oversight and individual state pawn and lending laws in each market it serves.

TitleMax's core product is the title pawn or title loan — a short-term loan secured by the borrower's vehicle title (car, truck, or motorcycle). In Georgia and select states, these products are legally structured as "title pawns" rather than loans under state law. Loan amounts go up to $10,000, determined by the vehicle's appraised value, with personal loans up to $2,500 available in some states. Interest rates are extremely high: Georgia customers face up to approximately 187.5% APR annually, while Alabama and Texas customers can see rates approaching 300% APR as permitted by state law. Loan terms are typically 30-day contracts that can be renewed. No credit check is required — only vehicle ownership with a free and clear title determines eligibility. There is no bank account requirement, and approved customers can receive cash in as little as 30 minutes at a store location.

TitleMax's primary differentiator is accessibility for consumers shut out of traditional lending. With 900+ physical locations across 14 states, it operates one of the largest in-store footprints of any title lender in the country, making same-day in-person transactions feasible for many borrowers. The company also provides a mobile app (iOS confirmed) and an online customer portal for payment management, balance checks, and store location. Unlike a traditional pawn shop where the item is held as collateral, TitleMax borrowers retain full use and possession of their vehicle throughout the loan term.

TitleMax is a high-risk, last-resort lending option that carries serious financial hazards. APRs ranging from 119% to 300% are among the highest in consumer finance, and the 30-day rollover structure has been documented as a debt trap by ProPublica, Georgia Watch, and consumer advocates. In 2023, parent company TMX Finance LLC received a CFPB enforcement order for violating the Military Lending Act — specifically extending title loans to active-duty servicemembers at rates far exceeding the 36% Military APR cap. The company is not BBB accredited and complaint volumes across its locations are substantial. For a vehicle-owning borrower with no other options facing a true emergency, TitleMax provides fast access to funds — but the true borrowing cost is extreme and vehicle repossession is a real and documented consequence of default.
address: 3665 Flat Shoals Rd Suite B, Decatur, GA 30034

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee offered. Early payoff options exist but no guarantee policy was found.

pros: No credit check required — vehicle value alone determines loan eligibility
Same-day cash disbursement in as little as 30 minutes at a store location
Borrower keeps and drives their vehicle throughout the entire loan term
No bank account required to qualify or receive funds
900+ physical store locations across 14 states for in-person access
Founded in 1998 with 25+ years of title lending operations
Personal loans up to $2,500 available in select states without vehicle collateral
cons: APR ranges from 119% to 300% depending on state — among the highest legally permitted in consumer lending
CFPB enforcement action in 2023 against parent TMX Finance LLC for Military Lending Act violations targeting active-duty servicemembers
30-day renewable loan terms are frequently rolled over, creating documented multi-month debt cycles with real risk of vehicle repossession
Not BBB accredited; multiple BBB profiles across locations reflect significant consumer complaint volumes
No longer originating new loans in California, Illinois, New Mexico, or Virginia due to regulatory pressure
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit history facing a genuine cash emergency
Consumers who have been declined by banks, credit unions, and personal loan lenders
People who own their car or truck free and clear (no existing lien) and need up to $10,000 quickly
diagnosis: TitleMax is best suited for vehicle-owning consumers with poor or no credit who face a genuine emergency and have exhausted conventional borrowing options. It provides fast, no-credit-check access to up to $10,000 secured by a vehicle title, with cash available in-store the same day. The central caveat is cost and risk: APRs between 119% and 300%, combined with 30-day rollover terms, make this one of the most expensive forms of consumer borrowing available, and vehicle repossession is a documented outcome for borrowers who cannot repay.
services: Car title pawns (Georgia and select states — legally distinct from loans under state pawn law)
Car title loans (secured by car or truck title)
Personal loans up to $2,500 (select states, no vehicle required)
Online loan application initiation via titlemax.com
Online customer portal for balance checks, payment history, and due dates
iOS mobile app for account management and store locating
In-store vehicle value appraisal to determine loan eligibility
